ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #11, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-03-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Mangini for Elizabeth Adler (Trustee).

Mr. Mangini stated that his fees were at a straight hourly rate, that the fees were reasonable and that he did not have a Paralegal as he was a sole practitioner. In addition, he stated that there were three buildings on 4 parcels, that one building had four units, that two of the units were being rented at a low rate to occupants of 20 years or longer, that the other two units had some turn over but rented out at fair market value, that one building was being occupied by the Beneficiary and that the last building was a former gas station/auto shop which needed a lot of repairs. Furthermore, he stated that the lot lines do not fit with the way the buildings were developed, that it would cost over $900,000.00 to bring everything up to code, and that special Counsel was meeting with the City and County and coming up with a proposal on changing the lot lines to try to sell the buildings. The Eleventh Account was taken under submission. Status was set for March 8, 2010 for filing of a declaration of Ms. Adler spelling out what has been accomplished and what needs to be accomplished to bring the property issue to a close. The Court stated that it would like to see timelines, that this matter had been going on for over 10 years, that Mr. Epstein had been paid $115,000.00 in attorney's fees and that it wanted an estimate as to how much in additional attorney's fees would be needed with regard to the property. The declaration is to be filed by February 26, 2010. Ms. Adler is required to appear on March 8, 2010.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
